confidence_rationale: Video is a conference presentation (UNeMed Demo Day 2015) showcasing the PortCAS (Portable Camera Aided System) platform for laparoscopic simulation training. Contains multiple speakers presenting the technology, team members, business roadmap, and platform analytics dashboards. This is not an FLS task performance video — it is educational/promotional content about the training platform itself, not a trainee performing an FLS task.
cannot_determine: No trainee performing Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture
Video shows platform interface, analytics, and presenters, not task execution
technique_summary: Not applicable — this is platform/product demonstration content, not task performance.

demonstrated_techniques: Cloud-based analytics and real-time feedback systems
Portability and accessibility of laparoscopic simulation
verbal_cues: PortCAS designed to provide collaborative training environment
Addresses cost and accessibility barriers in simulation-based training
Real-time feedback and performance analytics for learners
Platform extensible for multiple medical modules and curricula
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding expensive, inaccessible simulation equipment by using portable, cost-effective platform
Enabling distributed learning with cloud-based analytics rather than localized systems

note: This video documents the PortCAS innovation demonstration at UNeMed Innovation Demo Day (October 21, 2015). It is NOT a performance of an FLS MISTELS task by a trainee or expert, and therefore cannot be scored using FLS rubrics. If you have an actual FLS task performance video (showing someone performing Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture), please submit that for evaluation.
